audit_items.proof_trace: All dynamic allocations in lag_conf.c are properly freed on every path: (1) nfp_fl_lag_group structs (kmalloc, L122) are freed either in nfp_fl_lag_do_work after successful HW delete with to_destroy=true (L300-305) or unconditionally in nfp_flower_lag_cleanup (L686-689) after cancel_delayed_work_sync; (2) acti_netdevs arrays (kmalloc_array, L309) are freed at L352 (early-exit path) and L367 (main path), covering all control flow branches; (3) skbs from nfp_flower_cmsg_alloc (L214) have ownership transferred to nfp_ctrl_tx (L260), following standard kernel convention; (4) IDA resources are released via ida_simple_remove on every error/destroy path and ida_destroy at cleanup (L692).
